insights: Pattern: Hierarchical memory blocks with / naming (project/memory/repo-memory-link.md) is more scannable than flat files
Pattern: Skills should have scripts in scripts/ subdirectory for progressive disclosure
Pattern: Agent definitions in .claude/agents/ with YAML frontmatter for configuration
Decision: Use manual session-end trigger instead of automatic hook for user control
Decision: Bidirectional sync between Letta blocks and repo .memory/ for unified memory
next_steps: Test session start restoration with new memory blocks
Update CLAUDE.md with memory usage documentation
Consider adding semantic search over memories

insights: Neo4j/Graphiti is strictly a developer-facing tool, not exposed to users

insights: Pattern: Cross-platform skills/agents should be copied to .claude/, .gemini/, .codex/ directories for universal availability
Decision: Shared scripts in scripts/memory/ work for all three assistants - no duplication needed
Pattern: Letta memory blocks use hierarchical / naming (e.g., project/memory/repo-memory-link.md) for better scannability
Learning: User values cross-platform consistency - wants same capabilities in Claude, Gemini, Codex
Decision: Manual session-end trigger gives user control over when memory is persisted
next_steps: Test session start with Claude Code to verify memory restoration
Test session start with Gemini to verify cross-platform memory
Test session start with Codex to verify cross-platform memory

Consider semantic search over memories for future enhancement

insights: Pattern: Gemini model correctly inherits cross-platform memory system
Pattern: RunShellCommand replaces Bash in Gemini toolset
